title: UAE’s biggest bank FAB Q2 profit up 4%, beats estimate
summary: The bank saw impairment charges jump 26% to AED950 million
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
FAB is 38%-owned by Abu Dhabi sovereign wealth fund Mubadala and 18%-owned by members of Abu Dhabi's ruling family. Image used for illustrative purpose. Image courtesy: Getty Images/ Jeff Kingma

The bank saw impairment charges jump 26% to AED950 million

Staff Writer ,  ZAWYA

The UAE's biggest lender, First Abu Dhabi Bank (FAB), reported a 4% rise year-on-year (YoY) in second-quarter 2026 net profit to AED5.72 billion ($1.55 billion), supported by stronger net interest income that offset higher impairment charges and a decline in non-interest income.
